author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter IV. 43–54.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Gospel Lesson of to-day follows that of yesterday, and this is the subject of our discourse.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this passage the meaning, indeed, is not difficult of investigation, but worthy of preaching, worthy of admiration and praise.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, in reciting this passage of the Gospel, we must commend it to your attention, rather than laboriously expound it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now Jesus, after His stay of two days in Samaria, “departed into Galilee,” where He was brought up.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the evangelist, as he goes on, says, “For Jesus Himself testified that a prophet hath no honor in his own country.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was not because He had no honor in Samaria that Jesus departed thence after two days; for Samaria was not His own country, but Galilee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whilst, therefore, He left Samaria so quickly, and came to Galilee, where He had been brought up, how does He testify that “a prophet hath no honor in his own country”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rather does it seem that He might have testified that a prophet has no honor in his own country, had He disdained to go into Galilee, and had stayed in Samaria.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now mark well, beloved, while the Lord suggests and bestows what I may speak, that here is intimated to us no slight mystery.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You know the question before us; seek ye out the solution of it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But, to make the solution desirable, let us repeat the theme.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The point that troubles us is, why the evangelist said, “For Jesus Himself testified that a prophet hath no honor in his own country.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Urged by this, we go back to the preceding words, to discover the evangelist’s intention in saying this; and we find him relating, in the preceding words of the narrative, that after two days Jesus departed from Samaria into Galilee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it for this, then, thou saidst, O evangelist, that Jesus testified that a prophet hath no honor in his own country, just because He left Samaria after two days, and made haste to come to Galilee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On the contrary, I should have thought it more likely, that if Jesus had no honor in His own country, He should not have hastened to it, and left Samaria.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if I am not mistaken, or rather, because it is true, and I am not mistaken; for the evangelist saw what he was saying better than I can see it, saw the truth better than I do, he who drank it in from the Lord’s bosom: for the evangelist is the same John who, among all the disciples, reclined on the Lord’s breast, and whom the Lord, owing love to all, yet loved above the rest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it he, then, that should be mistaken, and I right in my opinion?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rather, if I am piously-minded, let me obediently hear what he said, that I may be worthy of thinking as he thought.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear then, dearly beloved, what I think in this matter, without prejudice to your own judgment, if you have formed a better.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we have all one Master, and we are fellow-disciples in one school.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, then, is my opinion, and see whether my opinion is not true, or near the truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In Samaria He spent two days, and the Samaritans believed on Him; many were the days He spent in Galilee, and yet the Galileans did not believe on Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Look back to the passage, or recall in memory the lesson and the discourse of yesterday.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He came into Samaria, where at first He had been preached by that woman with whom He had spoken great mysteries at Jacob’s well.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After they had seen and heard Him, the Samaritans believed on Him because of the woman’s word, and believed more firmly because of His own word, even many more believed: thus it is written.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After passing two days there (in which number of days is mystically indicated the number of the two precepts on which hang the whole law and the prophets, as you remember we intimated to you yesterday), He goes into Galilee, and comes to the city Cana of Galilee, where He made the water wine.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And there, when He turned the water into wine, as John himself writes, His disciples believed on Him; but, of course, the house was full with a crowd of guests.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So great a miracle was wrought, and yet only His disciples believed on Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has now returned to this city of Galilee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And, behold, a certain ruler, whose son was sick, came to Him, and began to beseech Him to go down” to that city or house, “and heal his son; for he was at the point of death.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did he who besought not believe?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What dost thou expect to hear from me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ask the Lord what He thought of him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Having been besought, this is what He answered: “Except ye see signs and wonders, ye believe not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He shows us a man lukewarm, or cold in faith, or of no faith at all; but eager to try by the healing of his son what manner of person Christ was, who He was, what He could do.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The words of the suppliant, indeed, we have heard: we have not seen the heart of the doubter; but He who both heard the words and saw the heart has told us this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In short, the evangelist himself, by the testimony of his narrative, shows us that the man who desired the Lord to come to his house to heal his son, had not yet believed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For after he had been informed that his son was whole, and found that he had been made whole at that hour in which the Lord had said, “Go thy way, thy son liveth;” then he saith, “And himself believed, and all his house.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now, if the reason why he believed, and all his house, was that he was told that his son was whole, and found the hour they told him agreed with the hour of Christ’s foretelling it, it follows that when he was making the request he did not yet believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Samaritans had waited for no sign, they believed simply His word; but His own fellow-citizens deserved to hear this said to them, “Except ye see signs and wonders, ye believe not;” and even there, notwithstanding so great a miracle was wrought, there did not believe but “himself and his house.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: At His discourse alone many of the Samaritans believed; at that miracle, in the place where it was wrought, only that house believed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is it, then, brethren, that the Lord doth show us here?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Galilee of Judea was then the Lord’s own country, because He was brought up in it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now that the circumstance portends something,—for it is not without cause that “prodigies” are so called, but because they portend or presage something: for the word “prodigy” is so termed as if it were porrodicium, quod porro dicat, what betokens something to come, and portends something future,—now all those circumstances portended something, predicted something; let us just now assume the country of our Lord Jesus Christ after the flesh (for He had no country on earth, except after the flesh which He took on earth); let us, I say, assume the Lord’s own country to mean the people of the Jews.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lo, in His own country He hath no honor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Observe at this moment the multitudes of the Jews; observe that nation now scattered over the whole world, and plucked up by the roots; observe the broken branches, cut off, scattered, withered, which being broken off, the wild olive has deserved to be grafted in; look at the multitude of the Jews: what do they say to us even now?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He whom you worship and adore was our brother.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And we reply, “A prophet hath no honor in his own country.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In short, those Jews saw the Lord as He walked on the earth and worked miracles; they saw Him giving sight to the blind, opening the ears of the deaf, loosing the tongues of the dumb, bracing up the limbs of the paralytics, walking on the sea, commanding the winds and waves, raising the dead: they saw Him working such great signs, and after all that scarcely a few believed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am speaking to God’s people; so many of us have believed, what signs have we seen?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is thus, therefore, that what occurred at that time betokened what is now going on.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Jews were, or rather are, like the Galileans; we, like those Samaritans.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have heard the gospel, have given it our consent, have believed on Christ through the gospel; we have seen no signs, none do we demand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, though one of the chosen and holy twelve, yet he was an Israelite, of the Lord’s nation, that Thomas who desired to put his fingers into the places of the wounds.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord censured him just as He did this ruler.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the ruler He said, “Except ye see signs and wonders, ye believe not;” and to Thomas He said, “Because thou hast seen, thou hast believed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had come to the Galileans after the Samaritans, who had believed His word, before whom He wrought no miracles, whom He without anxiety quickly left, strong in faith, because by the presence of His divinity He had not left them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now, then, when the Lord said to Thomas, “Come, reach hither thy hand, and be not faithless, but believing;” and he, having touched the places of the wounds, exclaimed, and said, “My Lord, and my God;” he is chided, and has it said to him, “Because thou hast seen, thou hast believed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, but “because a prophet has no honor in his own country?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But since this Prophet has honor among strangers, what follows?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are the persons here foretold; and that which the Lord by anticipation praised, He has deigned to fulfill even in us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They saw Him, who crucified Him, and touched Him with their hands, and thus a few believed; we have not seen nor handled Him, we have heard and believed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May it be our lot, that the blessedness which He has promised may be made good in us: both here, because we have been preferred to His own country; and in the world to come, because we have been grafted in instead of the branches that were broken off!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He showed that He would break off these branches, and ingraft this wild olive, when moved by the faith of the centurion, who said to Him, “I am not worthy that thou shouldest come under my roof; but only speak the word, and my child shall be healed: for I also am a man put under authority, having soldiers under me; and I say to one, Go, and he goeth; and to another, Come, and he cometh; and to my servant, Do this, and he doeth it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus turned to those who followed Him, and said, Verily I say unto you, I have not found so great faith in Israel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why not found so great faith in Israel?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because a prophet has no honor in his own country.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Could not the Lord have said to that centurion, what He said to this ruler, “Go, thy child liveth?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See the distinction: this ruler desired the Lord to come down to his house; that centurion declared himself to be unworthy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the one it was said, “I will come and heal him;” to the other, “Go, thy son liveth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the one He promised His presence; the other He healed by His word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The ruler sought His presence by force; the centurion declared himself unworthy of His presence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here is a ceding to loftiness; there, a conceding to humility.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As if He said to the ruler, “Go, thy son liveth;” do not weary me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Except ye see signs and wonders, ye believe not;” thou desirest my presence in thy house, I am able to command by a word; do not wish to believe in virtue of signs: the centurion, an alien, believed me able to work by a word, and believed before I did it; you, “except ye see signs and wonders, believe not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, if it be so, let them be broken off as proud branches, and let the humble wild olive be grafted; nevertheless let the root remain, while those are cut off and these received in their place.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where does the root remain?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the patriarchs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the people Israel is Christ’s own country, since it is of them that He came according to the flesh; but the root of this tree is Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, the holy patriarchs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And where are they?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In rest with God, in great honor; so that it was into Abraham’s bosom that the poor man, on being promoted, was raised after his departure from the body, and in Abraham’s bosom was he seen from afar off by the proud rich man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore the root remains, the root is praised; but the proud branches deserved to be cut off, and to wither away; and by their cutting off, the humble wild olive has found a place.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear now how the natural branches are cut off, how the wild olive is grafted in, by means of the centurion himself, whom I have thought proper to mention for the sake of comparison with this ruler.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Verily I say unto you, I have not found so great faith in Israel; therefore I say unto you, that many shall come from the east and from the west.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How widely the wild olive took possession of the earth!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This world was a bitter forest; but because of the humility, because of this “I am not worthy—many shall come from the east and from the west.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And grant that they come, what shall become of them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if they come, they are cut off from the forest; where are they to be ingrafted, that they may not wither?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And shall sit down,” saith He, “with Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: At what banquet, in case thou dost not invite to ever living, but to much drinking?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where, “shall sit down?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how will it be with them who came of the stock of Abraham?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What will become of the branches with which the tree was full?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What but to be cut off, that these may be grafted in?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Show us that they shall be cut off: “But the children of the kingdom shall go into outer darkness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore let the Prophet have honor among us, because He had no honor in His own country.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had no honor in His country, wherein He was formed; let Him have honor in the country which He has formed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in that country was He, the Maker of all, made as to the form of a servant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that city in which He was made, that Zion, that nation of the Jews He Himself made when He was with the Father as the Word of God: for “all things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of that man we have to-day heard it said: “One Mediator of God and men, the man Christ Jesus.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Psalms also foretold, saying, “My mother is Sion, shall a man say.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A certain man, the Mediator man between God and men, says, “My mother Sion.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why says, “My mother is Sion”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because from it He took flesh, from it was the Virgin Mary, of whose womb He took upon Him the form of a servant; in which He deigned to appear most humble.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “My mother is Sion,” saith a man; and this man, who says, “My mother is Sion,” was made in her, became man in her.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He was God before her, and became man in her.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who was made man in her, “Himself did found her; the Most High was made man in her most low.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because “the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He Himself, the Most High, founded her.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now, because He founded this country, here let Him have honor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The country in which He was born rejected Him; let that country receive Him which He regenerated.
